source: fastembed TextEmbedding.embed (BAAI/bge-base-en-v1.5); RDR-160 CA-1 spike 2026-06-15, +>512-token truncation entry 2026-06-15
texts: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.
Postgres with pgvector serves the nexus T3 vector store.

Multi
line
text with  weird   spacing and UNICODE: café résumé naïve.

Semantic retrieval systems map natural language questions onto dense vector representations. A vector database indexes those embeddings and answers nearest-neighbour queries efficiently. The bge family of models uses the classification token as the pooled sentence representation. Truncation at five hundred and twelve tokens bounds the context the encoder actually reads. Postgres with the pgvector extension stores embeddings alongside relational metadata columns. Cosine similarity between two L2-normalized vectors reduces to a single dot product. Parity between the Python reference and the Java service is the load-bearing correctness check. Onnxruntime executes the exported transformer graph without any Python interpreter in the loop. Tokenizers split raw text into wordpieces before the model ever sees a single integer identifier. The standard export avoids fused contrib operators that the Java runtime cannot execute.
